name: Python / SciPy
description: Python with the SciPy ecosystem (NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib, Pandas) is the most widely used open-source scientific computing stack. It is rapidly becoming the default language of physics.
whyUseIt: Python is free, open-source, and has an enormous ecosystem. NumPy handles arrays, SciPy provides scientific algorithms (integration, optimisation, FFT, linear algebra), Matplotlib produces publication-quality plots, and Pandas handles tabular data. The learning curve is gentle and the community is massive.
fieldPosition: Universal — used in every branch of physics from data analysis to simulation to machine learning
physicistWorkflow: Write analysis code in Jupyter notebooks, use NumPy for computation, Matplotlib for plotting, and SciPy for fitting and integration.
supervisorExpects: Clean, reproducible Python code with proper documentation and version control.
computationalImpact: Democratises scientific computing — no licence fees, runs anywhere, interfaces with C/Fortran for performance.
demoDescription: Fit a Lorentzian curve to spectral data, perform a chi-squared goodness-of-fit test, and produce a publication-ready plot with residuals.

vocabulary.definition: NumPy's multi-dimensional array object — the fundamental data structure
An interactive notebook environment combining code, output, and documentation

beginnerMistakes: Using Python lists instead of NumPy arrays for numerical work
Not vectorising operations (writing explicit loops instead of array operations)
